display_name: pyDREAM MonoSQ Normalized (Scaled Solvent Weight, Model Scale/Offset)
description:
pyDREAM MonoSQ Normalized (Scaled Solvent Weight, Model Scale/Offset)

Purpose:
Corrected scaled-solvent MonoSQ variant for workflows that need the global scale and additive offset to be applied explicitly to the model curve rather than to the experimental SAXS trace. Existing MonoSQ templates remain unchanged for project compatibility.

Structure Factor:
Monodisperse hard-sphere structure factor evaluated with calc_monodisperse_sq using the Percus-Yevick approximation. eff_r controls the effective hard-sphere radius and vol_frac controls the packing term.

Form Factor:
Weighted mixture of MD-derived SAXS component profiles assembled from the project's averaged cluster scattering curves. The weighted mixture is multiplied by the hard-sphere structure factor and combined with solv_w times the solvent trace before the global model transform is applied.

Model Equation:
I_raw(q) = sum_i w_i I_i(q) S_HS(q; eff_r, vol_frac) + solv_w * I_solv(q)
I_model(q) = scale * I_raw(q) + offset

Likelihood Convention:
The pyDREAM likelihood compares the unmodified experimental intensity values directly against I_model(q), with a point-normalized Gaussian log-likelihood. scale and offset are applied only inside the forward model.

Calculator Integration:
The solution-scattering estimator can pre-populate vol_frac from the physical solute-associated volume fraction and solv_w from the solvent-background multiplier. Since the solvent contribution is inside I_raw(q), the metadata marks the solvent branch as globally scaled.

Prefit Startup:
When this template is loaded with experimental data available, Prefit can apply its autoscale estimate to the scale and offset parameters unless a saved Best Prefit or current Prefit state already exists. Bounds are centered around the autoscale result.

Model Parameters:
w1, w2, ..., wN: Relative contribution of each MD-derived component profile.
solv_w: Solvent-background multiplier applied to the solvent trace before the model scale is applied using the user-configured Prefit/DREAM bounds.
offset: Additive model baseline applied after the model scale.
eff_r: Effective hard-sphere radius used in the structure-factor calculation.
vol_frac: Hard-sphere packing fraction used in the Percus-Yevick structure factor.
scale: Multiplicative factor applied to the raw model curve.
